headlineSummary: Anthropic skews negative on criticism of safety claims, reliability, and a degraded-performance incident, while OpenAI is more positive on useful workflow support; Gemini and Copilot sit in a close tie with limited evidence from the same disruption mention [E1] [E2] [E3] [E4] [E5].

ranking.summary: Anthropic is viewed negatively on trust, model reliability, and access/performance issues, though there is one positive note about a useful SVG workflow [E1] [E2] [E3] [E4] [E5].
Google Gemini has a neutral-to-mixed read with limited evidence, based on one disruption report in a close tie with Copilot [E4].
Microsoft Copilot also lands neutral-to-mixed with limited evidence, and it is in a close tie with Gemini from the same disruption report [E4].
OpenAI trends positive on a workflow comparison where GPT 5.5 is named as a replacement and Codex is credited with useful SVG generation [E2] [E5].

evidence.excerpt: So you can put Anthropic on your list of companies that like to talk big about safety, but when the rubber hits the road, profits matter more than safety.

annotations.rationale: Directly accuses Anthropic of hypocrisy on safety and prioritizing profit over safety.

evidence.excerpt: Limits were the last straw that made me cancel my subscription and make my workflow completely model agnostic with pi. While this is good news, I'm not coming back. Anthropic just lost me with too many wrongs in too short of a time period. Opus has been replaced with GPT 5.5, DeepSeek, Kimi, Qwen and they all allow me to use my own, single harness and switch models easily if any of them start treating me the same.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says Anthropic lost them and they are not coming back, citing too many problems and switching away from Opus.
GPT 5.5 is named as a satisfactory replacement in a favorable comparison against Opus.

evidence.excerpt: > I know full well that if you ask Claude Code to build a JSON API endpoint that runs a SQL query and outputs the results as JSON, it’s just going to do it right. It’s not going to mess that up. You have it add automated tests, you have it add documentation, you know it’s going to be good. I feel like this is just not true.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly rejects the claim that Claude Code reliably gets such tasks right.

evidence.excerpt: > Disruption with Gemini 2.5 Pro model > Disruption with Grok Code Fast 1 in Copilot > Incident with Copilot Grok Code Fast 1 > Claude Opus 4 is experiencing degraded performance It doesn't seem fair to blame Github for this? There's nothing they can do about it?

annotations.rationale: The excerpt explicitly reports a disruption affecting Gemini 2.5 Pro.
The excerpt explicitly reports incidents and disruption in Copilot availability.
The excerpt explicitly says Claude Opus 4 is experiencing degraded performance.

evidence.excerpt: But Claude or Codex is able to write SVG line by line (so you can make changes incrementally) and use Inkscape via CLI to generate icons, logos, and graphics for your app.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says Claude is able to perform a useful SVG-generation task effectively.
The commenter says Codex is able to perform the SVG-generation workflow effectively.
